skill.name: address-github-comments
skill.description: Use when you need to address review or issue comments on an open GitHub Pull Request using the gh CLI.
skill.summary: Use the GitHub CLI to efficiently address PR review comments and issue feedback systematically.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation for using the gh CLI. The detected 'external_commands' are markdown code formatting (backticks), not actual shell execution. No cryptographic algorithms or malicious code patterns present.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers systematically address PR review comments using the GitHub CLI, ensuring no feedback is missed and all issues are properly resolved.
actual_capabilities: Fetch and display PR review comments using gh pr view --comments
Categorize and plan fixes for each comment thread
Apply code changes to address specific feedback
Respond to comment threads as resolved using gh pr comment
limitations: Requires gh CLI to be installed and authenticated
Does not automatically apply fixes - requires human review
Cannot handle nested comment threads without user guidance

use_cases.title: Review Comment Workflow
use_cases.description: Systematically work through PR review comments one by one, ensuring each is addressed before marking as resolved.

use_cases.title: Issue Feedback Response
use_cases.description: Address feedback from GitHub issues by checking the associated PR and responding to thread comments.

use_cases.title: Batch Comment Management
use_cases.description: Handle multiple review comments efficiently by prioritizing and organizing the response workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Check PR Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the address-github-comments skill to fetch all comments for the current PR.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Comment Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: List all review comments and help me create a plan to address each one.

prompt_templates.title: Respond to Fixed Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: After I make changes, use the skill to respond to the resolved comment thread.

prompt_templates.title: Full Review Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the complete address-github-comments workflow: fetch comments, plan fixes, apply changes, and respond to threads.

output_examples.input: Run the address-github-comments skill to check my PR comments
output_examples.output:
Fetching comments for the current PR...

Found 3 review comments:

1. @reviewer1 - "Consider using const instead of let here"
   - Thread: src/utils.js:15
   - Status: Open

2. @reviewer2 - "This function should handle edge cases"
   - Thread: src/handler.js:42
   - Status: Open

3. @reviewer1 - "LGTM!"
   - Thread: src/utils.js:20
   - Status: Resolved

output_examples.input: I've fixed the issues, now respond to the comments
output_examples.output:
Responding to comment thread...

✓ Responded to src/utils.js:15
  Body: "Addressed in commit abc123 - changed let to const"

✓ Responded to src/handler.js:42
  Body: "Added edge case handling for null and undefined values"

best_practices: Always verify gh authentication with gh auth status before starting
Read surrounding code context before applying fixes to comments
Wait for user confirmation on which comments to address when there are many
anti_patterns: Applying fixes without understanding the full context of the comment
Responding to comments without actually making the requested changes
Skipping verification that gh is properly authenticated

faq.question: What is gh CLI?
faq.answer: gh is the official GitHub command-line tool for interacting with GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests from your terminal.

faq.question: Do I need to install gh CLI?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires gh CLI to be installed. You can install it from cli.github.com or via package managers like brew.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix code?
faq.answer: No, this skill helps you fetch and organize comments, but you must manually apply the code fixes yourself.

faq.question: What if I am not authenticated with gh?
faq.answer: Run gh auth login to authenticate with your GitHub account before using this skill.

faq.question: Does this work with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes, gh CLI supports GitHub Enterprise. Run gh auth login and select your enterprise instance.

faq.question: Can I respond to multiple comments at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can address multiple comments in sequence. The skill helps you track which comments have been resolved.
